NKJV.1: Praise the LORD! Praise the name of the LORD; Praise Him, O you servants of the LORD!
NKJV.2: You who stand in the house of the LORD, In the courts of the house of our God,
NKJV.3: Praise the LORD, for the LORD is good; Sing praises to His name, for it is pleasant.
NKJV.4: For the LORD has chosen Jacob for Himself, Israel for His special treasure.
NKJV.5: For I know that the LORD is great, And our Lord is above all gods.
NKJV.6: Whatever the LORD pleases He does, In heaven and in earth, In the seas and in all deep places.
NKJV.7: He causes the vapors to ascend from the ends of the earth;  He makes lightning for the rain; He brings the wind out of His treasuries.
NKJV.8: He destroyed the firstborn of Egypt, Both of man and beast.
NKJV.9: He sent signs and wonders into the midst of you, O Egypt,  Upon Pharaoh and all his servants.
NKJV.10: He defeated many nations And slew mighty kings—
NKJV.11: Sihon king of the Amorites, Og king of Bashan, And all the kingdoms of Canaan—
NKJV.12: And gave their land as a heritage, A heritage to Israel His people.
NKJV.13: Your name, O LORD, endures forever, Your fame, O LORD, throughout all generations.
NKJV.14: For the LORD will judge His people, And He will have compassion on His servants.
NKJV.15: The idols of the nations are silver and gold, The work of men’s hands.
NKJV.16: They have mouths, but they do not speak; Eyes they have, but they do not see;
NKJV.17: They have ears, but they do not hear; Nor is there any breath in their mouths.
NKJV.18: Those who make them are like them; So is everyone who trusts in them.
NKJV.19: Bless the LORD, O house of Israel! Bless the LORD, O house of Aaron!
NKJV.20: Bless the LORD, O house of Levi! You who fear the LORD, bless the LORD!
NKJV.21: Blessed be the LORD out of Zion, Who dwells in Jerusalem! Praise the LORD!

KJV.16: They have mouths<S>6310</S>, but they speak<S>1696</S> not; eyes<S>5869</S> have they, but they see<S>7200</S> not;

ESV.1: Praise the LORD! Praise the name of the LORD, give praise, O servants of the LORD,
ESV.2: who stand in the house of the LORD, in the courts of the house of our God!
ESV.3: Praise the LORD, for the LORD is good; sing to his name, for it is pleasant!
ESV.4: For the LORD has chosen Jacob for himself, Israel as his own possession.
ESV.5: For I know that the LORD is great, and that our Lord is above all gods.
ESV.6: Whatever the LORD pleases, he does, in heaven and on earth, in the seas and all deeps.
ESV.7: He it is who makes the clouds rise at the end of the earth, who makes lightnings for the rain and brings forth the wind from his storehouses.
ESV.8: He it was who struck down the firstborn of Egypt, both of man and of beast;
ESV.9: who in your midst, O Egypt, sent signs and wonders against Pharaoh and all his servants;
ESV.10: who struck down many nations and killed mighty kings,
ESV.11: Sihon, king of the Amorites, and Og, king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan,
ESV.12: and gave their land as a heritage, a heritage to his people Israel.
ESV.13: Your name, O LORD, endures forever, your renown,  O LORD, throughout all ages.
ESV.14: For the LORD will vindicate his people and have compassion on his servants.
ESV.15: The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the work of human hands.
ESV.16: They have mouths, but do not speak; they have eyes, but do not see;
ESV.17: they have ears, but do not hear, nor is there any breath in their mouths.
ESV.18: Those who make them become like them, so do all who trust in them.
ESV.19: O house of Israel, bless the LORD! O house of Aaron, bless the LORD!
ESV.20: O house of Levi, bless the LORD! You who fear the LORD, bless the LORD!
ESV.21: Blessed be the LORD from Zion, he who dwells in Jerusalem! Praise the LORD!

NASB.1: Praise the LORD! Praise the name of the LORD; Praise [Him], O servants of the LORD,
NASB.2: You who stand in the house of the LORD, In the courts of the house of our God!
NASB.3: Praise the LORD, for the LORD is good; Sing praises to His name, for it is lovely.
NASB.4: For the LORD has chosen Jacob for Himself, Israel for His own possession.
NASB.5: For I know that the LORD is great And that our Lord is above all gods.
NASB.6: Whatever the LORD pleases, He does, In heaven and in earth, in the seas and in all deeps.
NASB.7: He causes the vapors to ascend from the ends of the earth; Who makes lightnings for the rain, Who brings forth the wind from His treasuries.
NASB.8: He smote the firstborn of Egypt, Both of man and beast.
NASB.9: He sent signs and wonders into your midst, O Egypt, Upon Pharaoh and all his servants.
NASB.10: He smote many nations And slew mighty kings,
NASB.11: Sihon, king of the Amorites, And Og, king of Bashan, And all the kingdoms of Canaan;
NASB.12: And He gave their land as a heritage, A heritage to Israel His people.
NASB.13: Your name, O LORD, is everlasting, Your remembrance, O LORD, throughout all generations.
NASB.14: For the LORD will judge His people And will have compassion on His servants.
NASB.15: The idols of the nations are [but] silver and gold, The work of man's hands.
NASB.16: They have mouths, but they do not speak; They have eyes, but they do not see;
NASB.17: They have ears, but they do not hear, Nor is there any breath at all in their mouths.
NASB.18: Those who make them will be like them, [Yes], everyone who trusts in them.
NASB.19: O house of Israel, bless the LORD; O house of Aaron, bless the LORD;
NASB.20: O house of Levi, bless the LORD; You who revere the LORD, bless the LORD.
NASB.21: Blessed be the LORD from Zion, Who dwells in Jerusalem. Praise the LORD!

NLT.1:
Praise the LORD!
Praise the name of the LORD!
Praise him, you who serve the LORD,
NLT.2:
you who serve in the house of the LORD,
in the courts of the house of our God.
NLT.3:
Praise the LORD, for the LORD is good;
celebrate his lovely name with music.
NLT.4:
For the LORD has chosen Jacob for himself,
Israel for his own special treasure.
NLT.5:
I know the greatness of the LORD —
that our Lord is greater than any other god.
NLT.6:
The LORD does whatever pleases him
throughout all heaven and earth,
and on the seas and in their depths.
NLT.7:
He causes the clouds to rise over the whole earth.
He sends the lightning with the rain
and releases the wind from his storehouses.
NLT.8:
He destroyed the firstborn in each Egyptian home,
both people and animals.
NLT.9:
He performed miraculous signs and wonders in Egypt
against Pharaoh and all his people.
NLT.10:
He struck down great nations
and slaughtered mighty kings —
NLT.11:
Sihon king of the Amorites,
Og king of Bashan,
and all the kings of Canaan.
NLT.12:
He gave their land as an inheritance,
a special possession to his people Israel.
NLT.13:
Your name, O LORD, endures forever;
your fame, O LORD, is known to every generation.
NLT.14:
For the LORD will give justice to his people
and have compassion on his servants.
NLT.15:
The idols of the nations are merely things of silver and gold,
shaped by human hands.
NLT.16:
They have mouths but cannot speak,
and eyes but cannot see.
NLT.17:
They have ears but cannot hear,
and mouths but cannot breathe.
NLT.18:
And those who make idols are just like them,
as are all who trust in them.
NLT.19:
O Israel, praise the LORD!
O priests — descendants of Aaron — praise the LORD!
NLT.20:
O Levites, praise the LORD!
All you who fear the LORD, praise the LORD!
NLT.21:
The LORD be praised from Zion,
for he lives here in Jerusalem.
Praise the LORD!

WEB.1: Praise Yah! Praise Yahweh’s name! Praise him, you servants of Yahweh,
WEB.2: you who stand in Yahweh’s house, in the courts of our God’s house.
WEB.3: Praise Yah, for Yahweh is good. Sing praises to his name, for that is pleasant.
WEB.4: For Yah has chosen Jacob for himself; Israel for his own possession.
WEB.5: For I know that Yahweh is great, that our Lord is above all gods.
WEB.6: Whatever Yahweh pleased, that he has done, in heaven and in earth, in the seas and in all deeps;
WEB.7: who causes the clouds to rise from the ends of the earth; who makes lightnings with the rain; who brings the wind out of his treasuries;
WEB.8: Who struck the firstborn of Egypt, both of man and animal;
WEB.9: Who sent signs and wonders into the middle of you, Egypt, on Pharaoh, and on all his servants;
WEB.10: who struck many nations, and killed mighty kings,
WEB.11: Sihon king of the Amorites, Og king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan,
WEB.12: and gave their land for a heritage, a heritage to Israel, his people.
WEB.13: Your name, Yahweh, endures forever; your renown, Yahweh, throughout all generations.
WEB.14: For Yahweh will judge his people, and have compassion on his servants.
WEB.15: The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the work of men’s hands.
WEB.16: They have mouths, but they can’t speak. They have eyes, but they can’t see.
WEB.17: They have ears, but they can’t hear; neither is there any breath in their mouths.
WEB.18: Those who make them will be like them; yes, everyone who trusts in them.
WEB.19: House of Israel, praise Yahweh! House of Aaron, praise Yahweh!
WEB.20: House of Levi, praise Yahweh! You who fear Yahweh, praise Yahweh!
WEB.21: Blessed be Yahweh from Zion, Who dwells at Jerusalem. Praise Yah!

CSB17.1:

who stand in the house of the LORD,

AMP.1:
Praise the LORD! (Hallelujah!)
Praise the name of the LORD;
Praise Him, O servants of the LORD (priests, Levites),
AMP.2:
You who stand in the house of the LORD,
In the courts of the house of our God,
AMP.3:
Praise the LORD, for the LORD is good;
Sing praises to His name, for it is gracious and lovely.
AMP.4:
For the LORD has chosen [the descendants of] Jacob for Himself,
Israel for His own special treasure and possession.
AMP.5:
For I know that the LORD is great
And that our Lord is above all gods.
AMP.6:
Whatever the LORD pleases, He does,
In the heavens and on the earth, in the seas and all deeps—
AMP.7:
Who causes the clouds to rise from the ends of the earth;
Who makes lightning for the rain,
Who brings the wind from His storehouses;
AMP.8:
Who struck the firstborn of Egypt,
Both of man and animal;
AMP.9:
Who sent signs and wonders into your midst, O Egypt,
Upon Pharaoh and all his servants.
AMP.10:
Who struck many nations
And killed mighty kings,
AMP.11:
Sihon, king of the Amorites,
Og, king of Bashan,
And all the kingdoms of Canaan;
AMP.12:
And He gave their land as a heritage,
A heritage to Israel His people.
AMP.13:
Your name, O LORD, endures forever,
Your fame and remembrance, O LORD, [endures] throughout all generations.
AMP.14:
For the LORD will judge His people
And He will have compassion on His servants [revealing His mercy].
AMP.15:
The idols of the nations are silver and gold,
The work of men's hands.
AMP.16:
They have mouths, but they do not speak;
They have eyes, but they do not see;
AMP.17:
They have ears, but they do not hear,
Nor is there any breath in their mouths.
AMP.18:
Those who make idols are like them [absolutely worthless—spiritually blind, deaf, and powerless];
So is everyone who trusts in and relies on them.
AMP.19:
O house of Israel, bless and praise the LORD [with gratitude];
O house of Aaron, bless the LORD;
AMP.20:
O house of Levi, bless the LORD;
You who fear the LORD [and worship Him with obedience], bless the LORD [with grateful praise]!
AMP.21:
Blessed be the LORD from Zion,
Who dwells [with us] at Jerusalem.
Praise the LORD! (Hallelujah!)

MSG.1:
Hallelujah!
Praise the name of God,
praise the works of God.
MSG.2:
All you priests on duty in God's temple,
serving in the sacred halls of our God,
MSG.3:
Shout "Hallelujah!" because God's so good,
sing anthems to his beautiful name.
MSG.4:
And why? Because God chose Jacob,
embraced Israel as a prize possession.
MSG.5:
I too give witness to the greatness of God,
our Lord, high above all other gods.
MSG.6:
He does just as he pleases—
however, wherever, whenever.
MSG.7:
He makes the weather—clouds and thunder,
lightning and rain, wind pouring out of the north.
MSG.8:
He struck down the Egyptian firstborn,
both human and animal firstborn.
MSG.9:
He made Egypt sit up and take notice,
confronted Pharaoh and his servants with miracles.
MSG.10:
Yes, he struck down great nations,
he slew mighty kings—
MSG.11:
Sihon king of the Amorites, also Og of Bashan—
every last one of the Canaanite kings!
MSG.12:
Then he turned their land over to Israel,
a gift of good land to his people.
MSG.13:
God, your name is eternal,
God, you'll never be out-of-date.
MSG.14:
God stands up for his people,
God holds the hands of his people.
MSG.15:
The gods of the godless nations are mere trinkets,
made for quick sale in the markets:
MSG.16:
Chiseled mouths that can't talk,
painted eyes that can't see,
MSG.17:
Carved ears that can't hear—
dead wood! cold metal!
MSG.18:
Those who make and trust them
become like them.
MSG.19:
Family of Israel, bless God!
Family of Aaron, bless God!
MSG.20:
Family of Levi, bless God!
You who fear God, bless God!
MSG.21:
Oh, blessed be God of Zion,
First Citizen of Jerusalem!
Hallelujah!

NIV.1:
Psalm 135
Praise the Lord.
Praise the name of the Lord;
praise him, you servants of the Lord,
NIV.2:
you who minister in the house of the Lord,
in the courts of the house of our God.
NIV.3:
Praise the Lord, for the Lord is good;
sing praise to his name, for that is pleasant.
NIV.4:
For the Lord has chosen Jacob to be his own,
Israel to be his treasured possession.
NIV.5:
I know that the Lord is great,
that our Lord is greater than all gods.
NIV.6:
The Lord does whatever pleases him,
in the heavens and on the earth,
in the seas and all their depths.
NIV.7:
He makes clouds rise from the ends of the earth;
he sends lightning with the rain
and brings out the wind from his storehouses.
NIV.8:
He struck down the firstborn of Egypt,
the firstborn of men and animals.
NIV.9:
He sent his signs and wonders into your midst, O Egypt,
against Pharaoh and all his servants.
NIV.10:
He struck down many nations
and killed mighty kings—
NIV.11:
Sihon king of the Amorites,
Og king of Bashan
and all the kings of Canaan—
NIV.12:
and he gave their land as an inheritance,
an inheritance to his people Israel.
NIV.13:
Your name, O Lord, endures forever,
your renown, O Lord, through all generations.
NIV.14:
For the Lord will vindicate his people
and have compassion on his servants.
NIV.15:
The idols of the nations are silver and gold,
made by the hands of men.
NIV.16:
They have mouths, but cannot speak,
eyes, but they cannot see;
NIV.17:
they have ears, but cannot hear,
nor is there breath in their mouths.
NIV.18:
Those who make them will be like them,
and so will all who trust in them.
NIV.19:
O house of Israel, praise the Lord;
O house of Aaron, praise the Lord;
NIV.20:
O house of Levi, praise the Lord;
you who fear him, praise the Lord.
NIV.21:
Praise be to the Lord from Zion,
to him who dwells in Jerusalem.
Praise the Lord.

NRSVCE.1:
Praise the LORD!
Praise the name of the LORD;
give praise, O servants of the LORD,
NRSVCE.2:
you that stand in the house of the LORD,
in the courts of the house of our God.
NRSVCE.3:
Praise the LORD, for the LORD is good;
sing to his name, for he is gracious.
NRSVCE.4:
For the LORD has chosen Jacob for himself,
Israel as his own possession.
NRSVCE.5:
For I know that the LORD is great;
our Lord is above all gods.
NRSVCE.6:
Whatever the LORD pleases he does,
in heaven and on earth,
in the seas and all deeps.
NRSVCE.7:
He it is who makes the clouds rise at the end of the earth;
he makes lightnings for the rain
and brings out the wind from his storehouses.
NRSVCE.8:
He it was who struck down the firstborn of Egypt,
both human beings and animals;
NRSVCE.9:
he sent signs and wonders
into your midst, O Egypt,
against Pharaoh and all his servants.
NRSVCE.10:
He struck down many nations
and killed mighty kings —
NRSVCE.11:
Sihon, king of the Amorites,
and Og, king of Bashan,
and all the kingdoms of Canaan —
NRSVCE.12:
and gave their land as a heritage,
a heritage to his people Israel.
NRSVCE.13:
Your name, O LORD, endures forever,
your renown, O LORD, throughout all ages.
NRSVCE.14:
For the LORD will vindicate his people,
and have compassion on his servants.
NRSVCE.15:
The idols of the nations are silver and gold,
the work of human hands.
NRSVCE.16:
They have mouths, but they do not speak;
they have eyes, but they do not see;
NRSVCE.17:
they have ears, but they do not hear,
and there is no breath in their mouths.
NRSVCE.18:
Those who make them
and all who trust them
shall become like them.
NRSVCE.19:
O house of Israel, bless the LORD!
O house of Aaron, bless the LORD!
NRSVCE.20:
O house of Levi, bless the LORD!
You that fear the LORD, bless the LORD!
NRSVCE.21:
Blessed be the LORD from Zion,
he who resides in Jerusalem.
Praise the LORD!

NET.1: Praise the LORD! Praise the name of the LORD! Offer praise, you servants of the LORD,
NET.2: who serve in the LORD’s temple, in the courts of the temple of our God.
NET.3: Praise the LORD, for the LORD is good! Sing praises to his name, for it is pleasant!
NET.4: Indeed, the LORD has chosen Jacob for himself, Israel to be his special possession.
NET.5: Yes, I know the LORD is great, and our Lord is superior to all gods.
NET.6: He does whatever he pleases in heaven and on earth, in the seas and all the ocean depths.
NET.7: He causes the clouds to arise from the end of the earth, makes lightning bolts accompany the rain, and brings the wind out of his storehouses.
NET.8: He struck down the firstborn of Egypt, including both men and animals.
NET.9: He performed awesome deeds and acts of judgment in your midst, O Egypt, against Pharaoh and all his servants.
NET.10: He defeated many nations, and killed mighty kings —
NET.11: Sihon, king of the Amorites, and Og, king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan.
NET.12: He gave their land as an inheritance, as an inheritance to Israel his people.
NET.13: O LORD, your name endures, your reputation, O LORD, lasts.
NET.14: For the LORD vindicates his people, and has compassion on his servants.
NET.15: The nations’ idols are made of silver and gold, they are man-made.
NET.16: They have mouths, but cannot speak, eyes, but cannot see,
NET.17: and ears, but cannot hear. Indeed, they cannot breathe.
NET.18: Those who make them will end up like them, as will everyone who trusts in them.
NET.19: O family of Israel, praise the LORD! O family of Aaron, praise the LORD!
NET.20: O family of Levi, praise the LORD! You loyal followers of the LORD, praise the LORD!
NET.21: The LORD deserves praise in Zion — he who dwells in Jerusalem. Praise the LORD!
